steps.title: Identify what makes a comparison effective
steps.content: The student needs to compare the sizes of the two mountain ranges. The notes provide specific area measurements: 1,360 mi² for Peloncillo-Pan Duro and 65 mi² for Big Hatchet. An effective comparison must use both numbers to show which is larger.

steps.title: Find the choice that delivers both measurements
steps.content: Choice B directly provides both measurements and explicitly states which mountain is larger (1,360 mi² vs 65 mi²). Choices A, C, and D either generalize to dozens of mountains, speak vaguely about size variation, or focus on other properties like environmental isolation instead. That matches choice B — B is correct.
